examples.sentence: 試験に合格した上は、パーティーを開こう。
examples.translation: Now that I've passed the exam, let's have a party.
examples.translationEn: Now that I've passed the exam, let's have a party.
examples.romaji: Shiken ni goukaku shita ue wa, paatii wo hirakou.

examples.sentence: この仕事を引き受けた上は、最後までやり遂げるつもりだ。
examples.translation: Now that I have taken on this job, I intend to see it through to the end.
examples.translationEn: Now that I have taken on this job, I intend to see it through to the end.
examples.romaji: Kono shigoto wo hikiuketa ue wa, saigo made yaritogeru tsumori da.

examples.sentence: 親切だと言われた上は、手伝ってあげよう。
examples.translation: Since you said I'm kind, I'll help you out.
examples.translationEn: Since you said I'm kind, I'll help you out.
examples.romaji: Shinsetsu da to iwareta ue wa, tetsudatte ageyou.

examples.sentence: 甘いものが好きな上は、このデザートをきっと気に入るでしょう。
examples.translation: Now that you like sweets, you'll surely enjoy this dessert.
examples.translationEn: Now that you like sweets, you'll surely enjoy this dessert.
examples.romaji: Amai mono ga suki na ue wa, kono dezaato wo kitto ki ni iru deshou.

examples.sentence: 彼は忙しい以上、手伝いに行っても無駄だろう。
examples.translation: Since he's busy, it would be pointless to try helping him.
examples.translationEn: Since he's busy, it would be pointless to try helping him.
examples.romaji: Kare wa isogashii ijou, tetsudai ni itte mo muda darou.

examples.sentence: 約束した以上、守るべきだ。
examples.translation: Since I made a promise, I should keep it.
examples.translationEn: Since I made a promise, I should keep it.
examples.romaji: Yakusoku shita ijou, mamoru beki da.

examples.sentence: 締め切りが近い以上、全力で仕事をしなければならない。
examples.translation: As long as the deadline is near, I have to work with all my might.
examples.translationEn: As long as the deadline is near, I have to work with all my might.
examples.romaji: Shimekiri ga chikai ijou, zenryoku de shigoto wo shinakereba naranai.

examples.sentence: 彼女は大学生である以上、勉強が優先されるべきだ。
examples.translation: Since she's a college student, studying should be her priority.
examples.translationEn: Since she's a college student, studying should be her priority.
examples.romaji: Kanojo wa daigakusei de aru ijou, benkyou ga yuusen sareru beki da.

examples.sentence: 卒業以来、彼女に会っていない。
examples.translation: I haven't seen her since we graduated.
examples.translationEn: I haven't seen her since we graduated.
examples.romaji: Sotsugyou irai, kanojo ni atte inai.

examples.sentence: 子供が生まれて以来、忙しくなった。
examples.translation: I've been busy ever since my child was born.
examples.translationEn: I've been busy ever since my child was born.
examples.romaji: Kodomo ga umarete irai, isogashiku natta.

examples.sentence: 東京に引っ越して以来、友達が増えた。
examples.translation: I've made more friends since I moved to Tokyo.
examples.translationEn: I've made more friends since I moved to Tokyo.
examples.romaji: Toukyou ni hikkoshite irai, tomodachi ga fueta.

examples.sentence: あの映画を見た以来、彼女は怖がりになった。
examples.translation: She's been easily frightened ever since she watched that movie.
examples.translationEn: She's been easily frightened ever since she watched that movie.
examples.romaji: Ano eiga wo mita irai, kanojo wa kowagari ni natta.

examples.sentence: 彼は試験勉強をがんばり切るつもりだ。
examples.translation: He intends to give his absolute all to exam study.
examples.translationEn: He intends to give his absolute all to exam study.
examples.romaji: Kare wa shiken benkyou o ganbari kiru tsumori da.

examples.sentence: この仕事を終わり切るまで、帰らないでください。
examples.translation: Please don't go home until you've completely finished this job.
examples.translationEn: Please don't go home until you've completely finished this job.
examples.romaji: Kono shigoto o owari kiru made, kaeranai de kudasai.

examples.sentence: 友達と喧嘩して、何も言い切れなかった。
examples.translation: I argued with my friend and couldn't say everything I wanted to say.
examples.translationEn: I argued with my friend and couldn't say everything I wanted to say.
examples.romaji: Tomodachi to kenka shite, nanimo iikirenakatta.

examples.sentence: 彼らは、そのゲームをやり切ることができました。
examples.translation: They were able to complete that game thoroughly.
examples.translationEn: They were able to complete that game thoroughly.
examples.romaji: Karera wa, sono geemu o yarikiru koto ga dekimashita.

examples.sentence: この車は速い反面、燃費が悪いです。
examples.translation: On the one hand, this car is fast, but on the other hand, it has poor fuel efficiency.
examples.translationEn: On the one hand, this car is fast, but on the other hand, it has poor fuel efficiency.
examples.romaji: Kono kuruma wa hayai hanmen, nenpi ga warui desu.

examples.sentence: 彼は頭がいい反面、運動が苦手です。
examples.translation: On the one hand, he is smart, but on the other hand, he's not good at sports.
examples.translationEn: On the one hand, he is smart, but on the other hand, he's not good at sports.
examples.romaji: Kare wa atama ga ii hanmen, undou ga nigate desu.

examples.sentence: 彼女は親切である反面、面倒見が良すぎることがある。
examples.translation: On the one hand, she is kind, but on the other hand, she can be too helpful.
examples.translationEn: On the one hand, she is kind, but on the other hand, she can be too helpful.
examples.romaji: Kanojo wa shinsetsu de aru hanmen, mendoumi ga yoi sugiru koto ga aru.

examples.sentence: この仕事は給料が高い反面、ストレスがたくさんあります。
examples.translation: On the one hand, this job pays well, but on the other hand, it's quite stressful.
examples.translationEn: On the one hand, this job pays well, but on the other hand, it's quite stressful.
examples.romaji: Kono shigoto wa kyuuryou ga takai hanmen, sutoresu ga takusan arimasu.

examples.sentence: この雑誌は若者向けです。
examples.translation: This magazine is aimed at young people.
examples.translationEn: This magazine is aimed at young people.
examples.romaji: Kono zasshi wa wakamono-muke desu.

examples.sentence: スーパーで家族向けの食品セットが売っています。
examples.translation: The supermarket sells food sets intended for families.
examples.translationEn: The supermarket sells food sets intended for families.
examples.romaji: Suupaa de kazoku-muke no shokuhin setto ga utte imasu.

examples.sentence: このアプリは初心者向けに作られています。
examples.translation: This application is designed for beginners.
examples.translationEn: This application is designed for beginners.
examples.romaji: Kono apuri wa shoshinsha-muke ni tsukurarete imasu.

examples.sentence: このレストランはビーガン向けのメニューが充実しています。
examples.translation: This restaurant has an extensive menu aimed at vegans.
examples.translationEn: This restaurant has an extensive menu aimed at vegans.
examples.romaji: Kono resutoran wa bi-gan-muke no menyu ga juujitsu shite imasu.

examples.sentence: 地震が来る恐れがあるため、避難所に行ってください。
examples.translation: Please go to the evacuation center because there is a risk of an earthquake.
examples.translationEn: Please go to the evacuation center because there is a risk of an earthquake.
examples.romaji: Jishin ga kuru osore ga aru tame, hinanjo ni itte kudasai.

examples.sentence: 彼は事故に遭う恐れがあるので、注意して運転してください。
examples.translation: There's a possibility he might have an accident, so please drive carefully.
examples.translationEn: There's a possibility he might have an accident, so please drive carefully.
examples.romaji: Kare wa jiko ni au osore ga aru node, chuui shite unten shite kudasai.

examples.sentence: この薬を飲みすぎると、副作用の恐れがある。
examples.translation: If you take too much of this medicine, there is a risk of side effects.
examples.translationEn: If you take too much of this medicine, there is a risk of side effects.
examples.romaji: Kono kusuri wo nomisugiru to, fukusayou no osore ga aru.

examples.sentence: 低い予算でこのプロジェクトを進めると、品質が低くなる恐れがある。
examples.translation: If we proceed with this project on a low budget, the quality may suffer.
examples.translationEn: If we proceed with this project on a low budget, the quality may suffer.
examples.romaji: Hikui yosan de kono purojekuto wo susumeru to, hinshitsu ga hikuku naru osore ga aru.

examples.sentence: 喉が渇く折には、このペットボトルの水を飲んでください。
examples.translation: When you get thirsty, please drink water from this bottle.
examples.translationEn: When you get thirsty, please drink water from this bottle.
examples.romaji: Nodo ga kawaku ori ni wa, kono pettobotoru no mizu o nonde kudasai.

examples.sentence: この地域に来る折には、この神社に必ずお参りしましょう。
examples.translation: When you visit this area, make sure to go to this shrine.
examples.translationEn: When you visit this area, make sure to go to this shrine.
examples.romaji: Kono chiiki ni kuru ori ni wa, kono jinja ni kanarazu omairi shimashou.

examples.sentence: 彼が元気じゃない折には、具合が悪いか尋ねてください。
examples.translation: If he doesn't seem well, please ask if he's feeling sick.
examples.translationEn: If he doesn't seem well, please ask if he's feeling sick.
examples.romaji: Kare ga genki ja nai ori ni wa, guai ga warui ka tazunete kudasai.

examples.sentence: チャンスがある折には、躊躇せずに行動しましょう。
examples.translation: When you have an opportunity, act without hesitation.
examples.translationEn: When you have an opportunity, act without hesitation.
examples.romaji: Chansu ga aru ori ni wa, chuucho sezu ni koudou shimashou.
